refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) and the end-to-end scope, focusing on communication latency as the critical factor for safe teleoperation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate it meets the ≤ 150 ms threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link performance to support latency requirements.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression to reduce payload size.
DP2: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in network scheduling.
DP3: Implement edge processing to reduce round-trip communication distance.
DP4: Use low-latency network protocols and QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in network scheduling.

potential_sources: Interaction between network scheduling and vehicle telemetry data streams.
Dependency on network link quality and safety alert messaging.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates teleoperation traffic prioritization, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports traffic prioritization and QoS.
Risk that network conditions may degrade beyond agreed parameters affecting latency.
Assumes teleoperation data can be identified and isolated for prioritization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP3 (edge processing) if network prioritization alone is insufficient to meet latency targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation data flow, including traffic prioritization and communication latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges real-time vehicle state and control data with the Vehicle module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with the physical network infrastructure to send and receive teleoperation data.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains multiple functions but less focused on teleoperation latency specifics.
Primarily responsible for vehicle control and telemetry; less suitable for network traffic prioritization.
Directly responsible for teleoperation communication management; best containment for DP2.
Focused on safety alerts and policies; not directly involved in communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP2 to the Teleoperation module centralizes teleoperation communication management, minimizing diffusion and improving latency control.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module telemetry data may introduce latency if interfaces are not optimized.
Network Link interface variability could impact latency despite prioritization.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure tight interface contracts and performance monitoring between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules; consider fallback mechanisms if network prioritization fails.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (traffic prioritization) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing teleoperation latency with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists due to dependency on Vehicle telemetry data and Network Link variability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and minimal diffusion, making it the best architectural fit.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools within the Teleoperation module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for latency management to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface performance between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate latency risks.
Plan for network condition variability in system design and testing.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to an appropriate IM with acceptable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and consistent network link performance to support latency requirements.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level or implementation detail.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports traffic prioritization and QoS.
Telemetry data can be reliably exchanged with minimal added latency.
open_risks: Network conditions may degrade beyond agreed parameters.
Interface latency between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
Performance data of traffic prioritization mechanisms.
